records.title: Chinatown shophouse retail
records.decision: Asking rent is above official benchmark.
records.reason: Current asking rent is estimated around 20% above the latest transaction-backed benchmark. Negotiate unless the unit has unusually strong frontage, F&B approval, or fit-out value.
drivers: Chinatown tourism and dining activity are supporting stronger asking rents.
Limited shophouse supply keeps comparable options tight.
Recent F&B approvals and frontage premiums can justify part of the gap.
Asking-rent momentum is above the transaction-backed benchmark.
records.daily: Chinatown shophouse retail median rent rose from S$9.20 psf in 2021 to S$12.80 psf in 2025. Current asking rent is S$15.40 psf, about 20% above the latest transaction-backed benchmark.
records.action: Negotiate below S$14.60 psf unless the unit has premium frontage, F&B approval, or unusually strong fit-out value.
records.sourceSummary: Official benchmark: URA-linked retail rent trend. Asking estimate: recent shophouse listing signals. Confidence reduced by shophouse classification complexity.
records.mobileSummary: Likely high. Negotiate below S$14.60 psf unless the frontage is unusually strong.

records.title: Orchard mall retail
records.decision: Asking rent is elevated but supported by prime-mall demand.
records.reason: Prime Orchard mall asking rent is above the official benchmark, but the gap is narrower than shophouse-heavy areas. Check floor level, frontage, MRT linkage, and footfall before accepting.
drivers: Prime mall frontage supports a higher rent premium.
Tourist spend and retail footfall can lift Orchard asking expectations.
Floor level, unit position, and tenant mix can explain wide rent dispersion.
Asking momentum should be checked against the latest transaction evidence.
records.daily: Orchard mall retail asking rents are tracking around 14% above transaction-backed benchmarks, with ground and basement units carrying the strongest premium.
records.action: Accept only if the unit has strong floor position, MRT linkage, frontage, and mall traffic evidence.
records.sourceSummary: Official benchmark: stronger prime retail transaction signal. Asking estimate: Orchard mall listing and prime-unit premium signals.
records.mobileSummary: Elevated but possible. Validate traffic and floor position before accepting above S$34.80 psf.

records.title: Serangoon HDB retail
records.decision: Asking rent looks high for Serangoon HDB retail.
records.reason: Current asking rent is above the HDB-linked heartland benchmark. Negotiate unless the shop has MRT spillover, corner frontage, food approval, or unusually strong daily traffic.

Comparable HDB-linked benchmarks suggest the premium needs negotiation.
Recent asking momentum should be validated against unit-specific footfall.
records.daily: Serangoon HDB retail asking rent is estimated near S$12.20 psf, about 34% above the latest HDB-linked benchmark. Current asking should be challenged unless the unit captures MRT and NEX spillover traffic.
records.action: Push back above S$10.90 psf unless the unit has MRT spillover, corner frontage, food approval, or very strong observed footfall.

records.title: Bukit Panjang retail
records.decision: Asking rent looks high for ordinary Bukit Panjang retail.
records.reason: Bukit Panjang supports a real residential catchment and some stronger node demand near Hillion, but the current spread still needs better proof on location and flow before it should be accepted.
drivers: Hillion and LRT-linked flow can justify a better suburban number.
The current spread still looks rich for plain estate rows.
Neighbourhood units should be negotiated back toward the benchmark range.
Demand support should be proven with footfall, not just catchment size.
records.daily: Bukit Panjang retail asking rent is estimated near S$10.70 psf, about 24% above the latest benchmark. Better Hillion-linked units can support more, but ordinary stock still looks expensive.
records.action: Check Hillion and interchange-linked premium above S$10.00 psf before accepting.
records.sourceSummary: Official benchmark: north-west suburban retail trend. Asking estimate: Hillion and Bukit Panjang listing signals.
records.mobileSummary: Likely high. Check Hillion-linked premium above S$10.00 psf.

records.title: Tiong Bahru shophouse retail
records.decision: Asking rent looks high but partly supported by strong Tiong Bahru shophouse demand.
records.reason: Tiong Bahru shophouses support a meaningful lifestyle premium from dining demand, destination traffic, and limited supply. The spread is still high enough that weaker side stretches should be negotiated down.
drivers: Lifestyle demand and destination dining support a real shophouse premium.
Supply is limited, especially for well-fronted streetfront stock.
The premium should be strongest on proven dining and lifestyle stretches.
Weaker side streets and shallow frontage should still be marked down.
records.daily: Tiong Bahru shophouse retail asking rent is estimated near S$21.60 psf, about 21% above the latest benchmark. The premium is believable on stronger lifestyle stretches, but weaker side streets still need negotiation.
records.action: Check street quality above S$20.40 psf unless the frontage sits on a proven lifestyle stretch.
records.sourceSummary: Official benchmark: fringe-city shophouse trend. Asking estimate: Tiong Bahru streetfront listing signals.
records.mobileSummary: Elevated but plausible. Check street quality above S$20.40 psf.
